items.title: Epstein files fallout: The high-profile people burned by past dealings with a predator
items.description: Jeffrey Epstein killed himself in a New York City federal jail in 2019. But more than six years later, people are still losing their jobs because of him.

items.title: Goldman Sachs delivers contrarian take on the economy
items.description: Forget all the recession chatter. Goldman Sachs (GS) CEO David Solomon feels the economic backdrop for 2026 looks strong. In a recent CNBC interview, the veteran banker feels the macro setup is “quite good,” pointing to robust fiscal support, incredible AI-driven capital investment, and a more ...

items.title: Trump Tariffs Backfired, 130,000 Jobs Added In January And More: This Week In Economy
items.description: Trump admits Fed chair mistake in 2017, US job revisions wipe out gains, China cuts Treasury buying, and Goldman spots memory chip winners.

items.title: AAAU vs. SGDM: Direct Gold Exposure or Gold Mining Companies?
items.description: These two ETFs focus on one of the most coveted precious metals in the world, yet their investment approaches differ.
